purpose: Provide one deterministic, byte-identical harness and artifact-derived budget authority for every shared-field comparison arm.

minimum_arm_roles.mechanism: One versioned field supplies retrieval, independent selection, and revision readouts.
The substrate is shared while retrieval, selection, and revision learned blocks allocate the same total mutable capacity separately.
A keyed or bitemporal hard revision filter precedes retrieval and dispatch scoring.
An unversioned shared-field control isolates the contribution of the version ledger.

event_protocol.request_idempotency: same request and intent returns the committed event; same request with different intent is rejected
event_protocol.replay: ordered canonical events deterministically derive the terminal arm-namespaced state digest
isolation: mutable state is cloned independently per named arm run
cross-arm event, parent, and state references are rejected
cache, state, and random-stream sharing is forbidden unless represented by an immutable hash-shared artifact

inventory_contract.immutable_head_like_component_exception: allowed only with a byte-identical shared_artifact_sha256

requirements: frozen starting artifact clones are isolated
all canonical event types replay deterministically
missing, reordered, tampered, duplicate-conflict, and cross-arm injections fail closed
all E1 exact dimensions and prior-artifact guards are artifact-derived and equal per task and split
capped resources are separately reported and within numeric caps when caps are supplied
parameter and state inventories reject hidden heads and duplicate allocations
evaluator and analysis code hashes are bound before any future run

future_scientific_join_inputs.owner: future reviewed protocol and LakatoTree registration

limitations: evaluator_sha256 and analysis_code_sha256 are content bindings supplied to the receipt builder; this slice does not open files or prove how those hashes were produced
the receipt proves engineering replay, isolation, accounting, and binding only; it does not authorize dataset generation, outcome access, or an efficacy run
falsifiers: An exact retry appends a second event or a conflicting retry is accepted.
Replay accepts a missing, reordered, tampered, or cross-arm event/reference.
Two arms pass when any required E1 exact dimension or source usage event is missing or unequal.
A learned block, mutable head-like state, or duplicate allocation escapes inventory accounting.
Unequal capped resources are treated as exact-dimension failure despite remaining within declared caps.
EXPERIMENT_HARNESS_PASS is interpreted as preregistration, efficacy, winner, production, or novelty evidence.
